name: SolarWinds / APT29 (SUNBURST) Supply-Chain Intrusion
description: Faithful ordering of the APT29/UNC2452 post-implant activity following the SolarWinds Orion supply-chain compromise: a stealthy, low-and-slow chain favoring in-memory execution and defense evasion. The real intrusion dwelled ~2 weeks before hands-on-keyboard activity and moved deliberately to avoid detection; dwell here is compressed for a live demo while preserving the stealth-first ordering (injection and registry persistence BEFORE noisy lateral movement).

kill_chain.note: PowerShell tasking from the SUNBURST backdoor after dormancy
In-memory DLL injection to run TEARDROP/Cobalt Strike without touching disk
Selective LSASS credential theft for onward access
Registry Run-key persistence disguised as a legitimate updater
Deliberate RDP movement to targeted high-value systems
Disable endpoint security tooling on key hosts to extend dwell
